summary: Today’s PaymentsDive recap: 8 new stories, with focus on product + fintech innovation.

articles.title: Durbin targets airline points with bill
articles.description: A Senate bill proposed by the Illinois Democrat would regulate airline loyalty programs, with increased disclosure requirements for the carriers.

articles.title: Branch enlists Stripe for digital wallet payouts
articles.description: The EWA provider is using Stripe’s infrastructure to offer another tool that enables companies and contractors to pay their workforces.

articles.title: Citi pursues payments advances
articles.description: Real-time payments, agentic commerce and stablecoin advances are all topics the bank is discussing with clients, a top executive said.

articles.title: Klarna struggles with loan loss accounting
articles.description: With an assertive push into banking, the buy now, pay later pioneer has expanded consumer lending – along with growth in its loss provisions.

articles.title: FIS leans into big bank strategy
articles.description: The bank technology and card processing company has renewed its focus on large bank clients in the battle against rivals like Fiserv.

articles.title: Amex floats first new business card in 7 years
articles.description: The bank is betting that a card without preset spending caps, plus 2% cash back, will win over businesses.

articles.title: Electronic Transactions Association acquires peer
articles.description: The trade group’s absorption of the American Transaction Processors Coalition will bolster its payments industry advocacy.

articles.title: Mastercard bets on AI virtual CFOs
articles.description: The company’s new “virtual CFO” tool is tailored for lean teams and designed to augment — not replace — human leadership, Mastercard’s Mark Barnett said.
